heading: Life insurance contract defined
label: 7702 Life insurance contract defined
notes: Amendments2020—Subsec. (b)(2)(A). , substituted “the applicable accumulation test minimum rate” for “an annual effective rate of 4 percent”.Pub. L. 116–260, § 205(a)(1)Subsec. (b)(3). , added par. (3).Pub. L. 116–260, § 205(a)(2)Subsec. (c)(3)(B)(iii). , substituted “the applicable guideline premium minimum rate” for “an annual effective rate of 6 percent”.Pub. L. 115–97, § 13517(a)(4)(A)Subsec. (f)(10). , added par. (10).Pub. L. 115–97, § 13517(a)(4)(B)1988—Subsec. (c)(3)(B)(i), (ii). , added cls. (i) and (ii) and struck out former cls. (i) and (ii) which read as follows:Pub. L. 100–647, § 5011(a)“(i) the mortality charges specified in the contract (or, if none is specified, the mortality charges used in determining the statutory reserves for such contract),“(ii) any charges (not taken into account under clause (i)) specified in the contract (the amount of any charge not so specified shall be treated as zero), and”.Subsec. (c)(3)(D). , added subpar. (D).Pub. L. 100–647, § 5011(b)Subsecs. (j), (k). , added subsec. (j) and redesignated former subsec. (j) as (k).Pub. L. 100–647, § 6078(a)1986—Subsec. (f)(1)(A). , substituted “less any excess premiums with respect to which there is a distribution described in subparagraph (B) or (E) of paragraph (7) and any other amounts received” for “less any other amounts received”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1825(b)(2)Subsec. (f)(7). , amended par. (7) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (7)(A), in general, read as follows: “In the event of a change in the future benefits or any qualified additional benefit (or in any other terms) under the contract which was not reflected in any previous determination made under this section, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, there shall be proper adjustments in future determinations made under this section.”, and par. (7)(B), certain changes treated as exchange, read as follows: “In the case of any change which reduces the future benefits under the contract, such change shall be treated as an exchange of the contract for another contract.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1825(b)(1)Subsec. (g)(1)(B)(ii). , amended cl. (ii) generally. Prior to amendment, cl. (ii) read as follows: “the amount of premiums paid under the contract during the taxable year reduced by any policyholder dividends received during such taxable year.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1825(c)

Effective Date, , , as amended by , title XVIII, §§ 1825(e), 1899A(69), , , 2848, 2962, provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title II, § 221(d)98 Stat. 772Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalExcept as otherwise provided in this subsection, the amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending and provisions set out as a note under ] shall apply to contracts issued after , in taxable years ending after such date.section 101 of this titlesection 101 of this titleDecember 31, 1984“(2)Special rule for certain contracts issued after .—june 30, 1984“(A).—General ruleExcept as otherwise provided in this paragraph, the amendments made by this section shall apply also to any contract issued after , which provides an increasing death benefit and has premium funding more rapid than 10-year level premium payments.June 30, 1984“(B).—Exception for certain contractsSubparagraph (A) shall not apply to any contract if—“(i)such contract (whether or not a flexible premium contract) would meet the requirements of section 101(f) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954],“(ii)such contract is not a flexible premium life insurance contract (within the meaning of section 101(f) of such Code) and would meet the requirements of section 7702 of such Code determined by—“(I)substituting ‘3 percent’ for ‘4 percent’ in section 7702(b)(2) of such Code, and“(II)treating subparagraph (B) of section 7702(e)(1) of such Code as if it read as follows: ‘the maturity date shall be the latest maturity date permitted under the contract, but not less than 20 years after the date of issue or (if earlier) age 95’, or“(iii)under such contract—“(I)the premiums (including any policy fees) will be adjusted from time-to-time to reflect the level amount necessary (but not less than zero) at the time of such adjustment to provide a level death benefit assuming interest crediting and an annual effective interest rate of not less than 3 percent, or“(II)at the option of the insured, in lieu of an adjustment under subclause (I) there will be a comparable adjustment in the amount of the death benefit.“(C)Certain contracts issued before .—october 1, 1984“(i).—In generalSubparagraph (A) shall be applied by substituting ‘’ for ‘’ in the case of a contract—September 30, 1984June 30, 1984“(I)which would meet the requirements of section 7702 of such Code if ‘3 percent’ were substituted for ‘4 percent’ in section 7702(b)(2) of such Code, and the rate or rates guaranteed on issuance of the contract were determined without regard to any mortality charges and any initial excess interest guarantees, and“(II)the cash surrender value of which does not at any time exceed the net single premium which would have to be paid at such time to fund future benefits under the contract.“(ii).—DefinitionsFor purposes of clause (i)—“(I).—In generalExcept as provided in subclause (II), terms used in clause (i) shall have the same meanings as when used in section 7702 of such Code.“(II).—Net single premiumThe term ‘net single premium’ shall be determined by substituting ‘3 percent’ for ‘4 percent’ in section 7702(b)(2) of such Code, by using the 1958 standard ordinary mortality and morbidity tables of the National Association of Insurance Commissioners, and by assuming a level death benefit.“(3).—Transitional rule for certain existing plans of insuranceA plan of insurance on file in 1 or more States before , shall be treated for purposes of section 7702(i)(3) of such Code as a plan of insurance on file in 1 or more States before , without regard to whether such plan of insurance is modified after , to permit the crediting of excess interest or similar amounts annually and not monthly under contracts issued pursuant to such plan of insurance.September 28, 1983September 28, 1983September 28, 1983“(4).—Extension of flexible premium contract provisionsThe amendments made by subsection (b) [amending and provisions set out as a note under ] shall take effect on .section 101 of this titlesection 101 of this titleJanuary 1, 1984“(5).—Special rule for master contractFor purposes of this subsection, in the case of a master contract, the date taken into account with respect to any insured shall be the first date on which such insured is covered under such contract.”

Treatment of Flexible Premium Contracts Issued During 1984 Which Meet New Requirements, as added by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title II, § 221(b)(3)Pub. L. 99–514, title XVIII, § 1825(d)100 Stat. 2848Oct. 22, 1986“Any flexible premium contract issued during 1984 which meets the requirements of section 7702 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now 1986] (as added by this section) shall be treated as meeting the requirements of section 101(f) of such Code.”

children.text: meets the cash value accumulation test of subsection (b), or

children.label: A
children.text: meets the guideline premium requirements of subsection (c), and

children.label: B
children.text: falls within the cash value corridor of subsection (d).

children.heading: Cash value accumulation test for subsection (a)(1)
children.label: b Cash value accumulation test for subsection (a)(1)

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A contract meets the cash value accumulation test of this subsection if, by the terms of the contract, the cash surrender value of such contract may not at any time exceed the net single premium which would have to be paid at such time to fund future benefits under the contract.

children.label: A
children.text: on the basis of interest at the greater of the applicable accumulation test minimum rate or the rate or rates guaranteed on issuance of the contract,

children.label: B
children.text: on the basis of the rules of subparagraph (B)(i) (and, in the case of qualified additional benefits, subparagraph (B)(ii)) of subsection (c)(3), and

children.label: C
children.text: by taking into account under subparagraphs (A) and (D) of subsection (e)(1) only current and future death benefits and qualified additional benefits.

children.label: A
children.text: an annual effective rate of 4 percent, or

children.label: B
children.text: the insurance interest rate (as defined in subsection (f)(11)) in effect at the time the contract is issued.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A contract meets the guideline premium requirements of this subsection if the sum of the premiums paid under such contract does not at any time exceed the guideline premium limitation as of such time.

children.label: A
children.text: the guideline single premium, or

children.label: B
children.text: the sum of the guideline level premiums to such date.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “guideline single premium” means the premium at issue with respect to future benefits under the contract.

children.heading: Basis on which determination is made
children.label: B Basis on which determination is made
i
children.text: reasonable mortality charges which meet the requirements prescribed in regulations to be promulgated by the Secretary or that do not exceed the mortality charges specified in the prevailing commissioners’ standard tables as defined in subsection (f)(10),

children.label: ii
children.text: any reasonable charges (other than mortality charges) which (on the basis of the company’s experience, if any, with respect to similar contracts) are reasonably expected to be actually paid, and

children.label: iii
children.text: interest at the greater of the applicable guideline premium minimum rate or the rate or rates guaranteed on issuance of the contract.

children.heading: When determination made
children.label: C When determination made
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (f)(7), the determination under subparagraph (A) shall be made as of the time the contract is issued.

children.heading: Charges not specified in the contract
children.label: i Charges not specified in the contract
children.text: If any charge is not specified in the contract, the amount taken into account under subparagraph (B)(ii) for such charge shall be zero.

children.heading: New companies, etc.
children.label: ii New companies, etc.
children.text: If any company does not have adequate experience for purposes of the determination under subparagraph (B)(ii), to the extent provided in regulations, such determination shall be made on the basis of the industry-wide experience.

children.heading: Applicable guideline premium minimum rate
children.label: E Applicable guideline premium minimum rate
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B)(iii), the term “applicable guideline premium minimum rate” means the applicable accumulation test minimum rate (as defined in subsection (b)(3)) plus 2 percentage points.

children.heading: Guideline level premium
children.label: 4 Guideline level premium
children.text: The term “guideline level premium” means the level annual amount, payable over a period not ending before the insured attains age 95, computed on the same basis as the guideline single premium, except that paragraph (3)(B)(iii) shall be applied by substituting “the applicable accumulation test minimum rate” for “the applicable guideline premium minimum rate”.

children.heading: Cash value corridor for purposes of subsection (a)(2)(B)
children.label: d Cash value corridor for purposes of subsection (a)(2)(B)

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A contract falls within the cash value corridor of this subsection if the death benefit under the contract at any time is not less than the applicable percentage of the cash surrender value.

children.heading: Applicable percentage
children.label: 2 Applicable percentage
children.text: In the case of an insuredwith an attained age asof the beginning of thecontract year of:The applicable percentageshall decrease by aratable portion for eachfull year:More than:But notmore than:From:To:040250250404525021545502151855055185150556015013060651301206570120115707511510575901051059095105100.

children.label: A
children.text: the death benefit (and any qualified additional benefit) shall be deemed not to increase,

children.label: B
children.text: the maturity date, including the date on which any benefit described in subparagraph (C) is payable, shall be deemed to be no earlier than the day on which the insured attains age 95, and no later than the day on which the insured attains age 100,

children.label: C
children.text: the death benefits shall be deemed to be provided until the maturity date determined by taking into account subparagraph (B), and

children.label: D
children.text: the amount of any endowment benefit (or sum of endowment benefits, including any cash surrender value on the maturity date determined by taking into account subparagraph (B)) shall be deemed not to exceed the least amount payable as a death benefit at any time under the contract.

children.heading: Limited increases in death benefit permitted
children.label: 2 Limited increases in death benefit permitted
A
children.text: for purposes of computing the guideline level premium, an increase in the death benefit which is provided in the contract may be taken into account but only to the extent necessary to prevent a decrease in the excess of the death benefit over the cash surrender value of the contract,

children.label: B
children.text: for purposes of the cash value accumulation test, the increase described in subparagraph (A) may be taken into account if the contract will meet such test at all times assuming that the net level reserve (determined as if level annual premiums were paid for the contract over a period not ending before the insured attains age 95) is substituted for the net single premium, and

children.label: i
children.text: has an initial death benefit of $5,000 or less and a maximum death benefit of $25,000 or less,

children.label: ii
children.text: provides for a fixed predetermined annual increase not to exceed 10 percent of the initial death benefit or 8 percent of the death benefit at the end of the preceding year, and

children.label: iii
children.text: was purchased to cover payment of burial expenses or in connection with prearranged funeral expenses.

children.heading: Other definitions and special rules
children.label: f Other definitions and special rules

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “premiums paid” means the premiums paid under the contract less amounts (other than amounts includible in gross income) to which section 72(e) applies and less any excess premiums with respect to which there is a distribution described in subparagraph (B) or (E) of paragraph (7) and any other amounts received with respect to the contract which are specified in regulations.

children.heading: Treatment of certain premiums returned to policyholder
children.label: B Treatment of certain premiums returned to policyholder
children.text: If, in order to comply with the requirements of subsection (a)(2)(A), any portion of any premium paid during any contract year is returned by the insurance company (with interest) within 60 days after the end of a contract year, the amount so returned (excluding interest) shall be deemed to reduce the sum of the premiums paid under the contract during such year.

children.heading: Interest returned includible in gross income
children.label: C Interest returned includible in gross income
children.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of section 72(e), the amount of any interest returned as provided in subparagraph (B) shall be includible in the gross income of the recipient.

children.heading: Cash surrender value
children.label: A Cash surrender value
children.text: The cash surrender value of any contract shall be its cash value determined without regard to any surrender charge, policy loan, or reasonable termination dividends.

children.heading: Net surrender value
children.label: B Net surrender value
children.text: The net surrender value of any contract shall be determined with regard to surrender charges but without regard to any policy loan.

children.heading: Death benefit
children.label: 3 Death benefit
children.text: The term “death benefit” means the amount payable by reason of the death of the insured (determined without regard to any qualified additional benefits).

children.heading: Future benefits
children.label: 4 Future benefits
children.text: The term “future benefits” means death benefits and endowment benefits.

children.label: i
children.text: guaranteed insurability,

children.label: ii
children.text: accidental death or disability benefit,

children.label: iii
children.text: family term coverage,

children.label: iv
children.text: disability waiver benefit, or

children.label: v
children.text: other benefit prescribed under regulations.

children.heading: Treatment of qualified additional benefits
children.label: B Treatment of qualified additional benefits
children.text: For purposes of this section, qualified additional benefits shall not be treated as future benefits under the contract, but the charges for such benefits shall be treated as future benefits.

children.heading: Treatment of other additional benefits
children.label: C Treatment of other additional benefits
i
children.text: such benefit shall not be treated as a future benefit, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any charge for such benefit which is not prefunded shall not be treated as a premium.

children.heading: Premium payments not disqualifying contract
children.label: 6 Premium payments not disqualifying contract
children.text: The payment of a premium which would result in the sum of the premiums paid exceeding the guideline premium limitation shall be disregarded for purposes of subsection (a)(2) if the amount of such premium does not exceed the amount necessary to prevent the termination of the contract on or before the end of the contract year (but only if the contract will have no cash surrender value at the end of such extension period).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If there is a change in the benefits under (or in other terms of) the contract which was not reflected in any previous determination or adjustment made under this section, there shall be proper adjustments in future determinations made under this section.

children.heading: Rule for certain changes during first 15 years
children.label: B Rule for certain changes during first 15 years
i
children.text: a change described in subparagraph (A) reduces benefits under the contract,

children.label: ii
children.text: the change occurs during the 15-year period beginning on the issue date of the contract, and

children.label: iii
children.text: a cash distribution is made to the policyholder as a result of such change,

children.heading: Recapture ceiling where change occurs during first 5 years
children.label: C Recapture ceiling where change occurs during first 5 years
I
children.text: the cash surrender value of the contract, immediately before the reduction, over

children.label: II
children.text: the net single premium (determined under subsection (b)), immediately after the reduction, or

children.label: I
children.text: the excess of the aggregate premiums paid under the contract, immediately before the reduction, over the guideline premium limitation for the contract (determined under subsection (c)(2), taking into account the adjustment described in subparagraph (A)), or

children.label: II
children.text: the excess of the cash surrender value of the contract, immediately before the reduction, over the cash value corridor of subsection (d) (determined immediately after the reduction).

children.heading: Recapture ceiling where change occurs after 5th year and before 16th year
children.label: D Recapture ceiling where change occurs after 5th year and before 16th year
children.text: If the change referred to in subparagraph (B) occurs after the 5-year period referred to under subparagraph (C), the recapture ceiling is the excess of the cash surrender value of the contract, immediately before the reduction, over the cash value corridor of subsection (d) (determined immediately after the reduction and whether or not subsection (d) applies to the contract).

children.heading: Treatment of certain distributions made in anticipation of benefit reductions
children.label: E Treatment of certain distributions made in anticipation of benefit reductions
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, subparagraph (B) shall apply also to any distribution made in anticipation of a reduction in benefits under the contract. For purposes of the preceding sentence, appropriate adjustments shall be made in the provisions of subparagraphs (C) and (D); and any distribution which reduces the cash surrender value of a contract and which is made within 2 years before a reduction in benefits under the contract shall be treated as made in anticipation of such reduction.

children.label: A
children.text: the requirements described in subsection (a) for any contract year were not satisfied due to reasonable error, and

children.label: B
children.text: reasonable steps are being taken to remedy the error,

children.heading: Special rule for variable life insurance contracts
children.label: 9 Special rule for variable life insurance contracts
children.text: In the case of any contract which is a variable contract (as defined in section 817), the determination of whether such contract meets the requirements of subsection (a) shall be made whenever the death benefits under such contract change but not less frequently than once during each 12-month period.

children.heading: Prevailing commissioners’ standard tables
children.label: 10 Prevailing commissioners’ standard tables
children.text: For purposes of subsection (c)(3)(B)(i), the term “prevailing commissioners’ standard tables” means the most recent commissioners’ standard tables prescribed by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners which are permitted to be used in computing reserves for that type of contract under the insurance laws of at least 26 States when the contract was issued. If the prevailing commissioners’ standard tables as of the beginning of any calendar year (hereinafter in this paragraph referred to as the “year of change”) are different from the prevailing commissioners’ standard tables as of the beginning of the preceding calendar year, the issuer may use the prevailing commissioners’ standard tables as of the beginning of the preceding calendar year with respect to any contract issued after the change and before the close of the 3-year period beginning on the first day of the year of change.

children.label: i
children.text: the section 7702 valuation interest rate for such calendar year (or, if such calendar year is not an adjustment year, the most recent adjustment year), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the section 7702 applicable Federal interest rate for such calendar year (or, if such calendar year is not an adjustment year, the most recent adjustment year).

children.heading: Section 7702 valuation interest rate
children.label: B Section 7702 valuation interest rate
children.text: The term “section 7702 valuation interest rate” means, with respect to any adjustment year, the prescribed U.S. valuation interest rate for life insurance with guaranteed durations of more than 20 years (as defined in the National Association of Insurance Commissioners’ Standard Valuation Law) as effective in the calendar year immediately preceding such adjustment year.

children.heading: Section 7702 applicable Federal interest rate
children.label: C Section 7702 applicable Federal interest rate
children.text: The term “section 7702 applicable Federal interest rate” means, with respect to any adjustment year, the average (rounded to the nearest whole percentage point) of the applicable Federal mid-term rates (as defined in section 1274(d) but based on annual compounding) effective as of the beginning of each of the calendar months in the most recent 60-month period ending before the second calendar year prior to such adjustment year.

children.heading: Adjustment year
children.label: D Adjustment year
children.text: The term “adjustment year” means the calendar year following any calendar year that includes the effective date of a change in the prescribed U.S. valuation interest rate for life insurance with guaranteed durations of more than 20 years (as defined in the National Association of Insurance Commissioners’ Standard Valuation Law).

children.label: i
children.text: begins on , andJanuary 1, 2021

children.label: ii
children.text: ends immediately before the beginning of the first adjustment year that beings after .1So in original. Probably should be “begins”.1December 31, 2021

children.heading: Treatment of contracts which do not meet subsection (a) test
children.label: g Treatment of contracts which do not meet subsection (a) test

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If at any time any contract which is a life insurance contract under the applicable law does not meet the definition of life insurance contract under subsection (a), the income on the contract for any taxable year of the policyholder shall be treated as ordinary income received or accrued by the policyholder during such year.

children.label: I
children.text: the increase in the net surrender value of the contract during the taxable year, and

children.label: II
children.text: the cost of life insurance protection provided under the contract during the taxable year, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the premiums paid (as defined in subsection (f)(1)) under the contract during the taxable year.

children.heading: Contracts which cease to meet definition
children.label: C Contracts which cease to meet definition
children.text: If, during any taxable year of the policyholder, a contract which is a life insurance contract under the applicable law ceases to meet the definition of life insurance contract under subsection (a), the income on the contract for all prior taxable years shall be treated as received or accrued during the taxable year in which such cessation occurs.

children.heading: Cost of life insurance protection
children.label: D Cost of life insurance protection
i
children.text: the cost of individual insurance on the life of the insured as determined on the basis of uniform premiums (computed on the basis of 5-year age brackets) prescribed by the Secretary by regulations, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the mortality charge (if any) stated in the contract.

children.heading: Treatment of amount paid on death of insured
children.label: 2 Treatment of amount paid on death of insured
children.text: If any contract which is a life insurance contract under the applicable law does not meet the definition of life insurance contract under subsection (a), the excess of the amount paid by the reason of the death of the insured over the net surrender value of the contract shall be deemed to be paid under a life insurance contract for purposes of section 101 and subtitle B.

children.heading: Contract continues to be treated as insurance contract
children.label: 3 Contract continues to be treated as insurance contract
children.text: If any contract which is a life insurance contract under the applicable law does not meet the definition of life insurance contract under subsection (a), such contract shall, notwithstanding such failure, be treated as an insurance contract for purposes of this title.

children.heading: Endowment contracts receive same treatment
children.label: h Endowment contracts receive same treatment

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: References in subsections (a) and (g) to a life insurance contract shall be treated as including references to a contract which is an endowment contract under the applicable law.

children.heading: Definition of endowment contract
children.label: 2 Definition of endowment contract
children.text: For purposes of this title (other than paragraph (1)), the term “endowment contract” means a contract which is an endowment contract under the applicable law and which meets the requirements of subsection (a).

children.heading: Transitional rule for certain 20-pay contracts
children.label: i Transitional rule for certain 20-pay contracts

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a qualified 20-pay contract, this section shall be applied by substituting “3 percent” for “4 percent” in subsection (b)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: requires at least 20 nondecreasing annual premium payments, and

children.label: B
children.text: is issued pursuant to an existing plan of insurance.

children.heading: Existing plan of insurance
children.label: 3 Existing plan of insurance
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “existing plan of insurance” means, with respect to any contract, any plan of insurance which was filed by the company issuing such contract in 1 or more States before , and is on file in the appropriate State for such contract.September 28, 1983

children.heading: Certain church self-funded death benefit plans treated as life insurance
children.label: j Certain church self-funded death benefit plans treated as life insurance

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In determining whether any plan or arrangement described in paragraph (2) is a life insurance contract, the requirement of subsection (a) that the contract be a life insurance contract under applicable law shall not apply.

children.label: A
children.text: such plan or arrangement provides for the payment of benefits by reason of the death of the individuals covered under such plan or arrangement, and

children.label: B
children.text: such plan or arrangement is provided by a church for the benefit of its employees and their beneficiaries, directly or through an organization described in section 414(e)(3)(A) or an organization described in section 414(e)(3)(B)(ii).

children.heading: Church
children.label: A Church
children.text: The term “church” means a church or a convention or association of churches.

children.heading: Employee
children.label: B Employee
children.text: The term “employee” includes an employee described in section 414(e)(3)(B).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: k Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section.
